original_hypothesis.title: setDistributableERC20s Callable During Active Distribution Causes Permanent Contract Lock
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: setDistributableERC20s has no check for LockedForDistribution. If the owner calls it during an active distribution (LockedForDistribution=true), the distributableERC20s array changes while erc20EntitlementPerUnit retains its old length. If the new array is LONGER than the old one, the inner loop in distribute() (`for j = 0; j < distributableERC20s.length; j++`) will access erc20EntitlementPerUnit[j] out of bounds, causing a permanent revert. Since LockedForDistribution is already true, the contract is permanently frozen — no transfers, mints, or burns possible.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a missing state guard: setDistributableERC20s should check !LockedForDistribution. The code path is: _beginDistribution sets erc20EntitlementPerUnit (length N), then admin calls setDistributableERC20s (length M > N), then distribute() tries erc20EntitlementPerUnit[N..M-1] which doesn't exist → revert. No recovery path exists.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setDistributableERC20s has onlyOwner but no LockedForDistribution check, while distribute() iterates distributableERC20s.length indexing into erc20EntitlementPerUnit

preconditions: New array is longer than the one used during _beginDistribution

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin operation during distribution permanently bricks contract
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No profit — permanent protocol destruction. Admin may do this accidentally.

economic_questions: Can _endDistribution be called to unlock if distribute() always reverts?
Is there any admin recovery mechanism?
What happens if the new array is shorter instead of longer?
validation_requirements: Test setDistributableERC20s during active distribution with longer array
Verify permanent revert in distribute() after array mismatch
Confirm no recovery path from locked state

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: 100% loss of tokens and revenue access

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Test both lengthening and shortening scenarios

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
